activities.primarySicName: Cottonseed Oil Mills; Soybean Oil Mills
activities.activityTypeDescription: If a facility generates air pollutants during operation of its business, it may need an air quality permit, which helps to minimize these pollutants by establishing limits, operating requirements and monitoring.

activities.primarySicName: Cottonseed Oil Mills; Soybean Oil Mills
activities.activityTypeDescription: Hazardous waste includes substances that are corrosive, explosive, toxic and-or fire hazards. Minimal quantity generators generate less than 100 pounds per year, none of which is classified as an acute hazardous waste.

activities.primarySicName: Cottonseed Oil Mills; Soybean Oil Mills
activities.activityTypeDescription: Integrated remediation sites typically involve integrating guidance and policies from multiple remediation programs. They tend to involve large complex facilities, or a large volume of contamination being released to the environment.

Leak sites are locations where a release of petroleum products has occurred from a tank system. Leak sites can occur from aboveground or underground tank systems as well as from spills at tank facilities.
activities.primarySicName: Cottonseed Oil Mills; Soybean Oil Mills
Cottonseed Oil Mills; Soybean Oil Mills
activities.activityTypeDescription: The federal toxics release inventory (TRI) tracks the management of certain toxic chemicals. In Minnesota, facilities reporting toxic chemical releases are required to pay pollution prevention fees to the MPCA. These fees are also assessed to hazardous waste large quantity generators.

activities.primarySicName: Cottonseed Oil Mills; Soybean Oil Mills
activities.activityTypeDescription: At industrial sites, stormwater may come into contact with harmful pollutants such as toxic metals, oil, grease, and de-icing salts. Industrial stormwater permits are designed to limit the contaminants that reach surface and groundwater.

An aboveground storage tank site has at least one tank of a certain size on the premises. A tank site may have multiple tanks and these tanks may contain food products, petroleum products, or other substances.

activities.activityTypeDescription: Industrial wastewater facilities may include factories, mines, and other privately owned facilities, as well as drinking water treatment plants and city pesticide application activities. Facilities that discharge directly to surface water require a NPDES/SDS permit, whereas those that do not may require an SDS permit.
